channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Germany Signals Openness to India Joining FCAS as Disputes Threaten Program Stability
item.description: India’s potential entry into the Franco-German-Spanish Future Combat Air System (FCAS) program is gaining strategic traction at a time when the ambitious sixth-generation fighter initiative is facing a decisive internal crisis. Signals emerging from German political and government circles suggest that Berlin has no fundamental objection to India joining the program, provided France and Germany resolve their ongoing disputes and finalize workshare arrangements. This development comes as FCAS enters what many analysts describe as a “make-or-break” phase.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Expands Lucknow Hub with New Wheels & Brake Shop to Support Tejas Mk1A/Mk2 and High-Tempo Operations
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has issued an open works tender (Ref: HAL-AD/9904/4789/CE/2026/) for constructing a dedicated Wheels and Brake shop at its Lucknow facility—an investment that signals a shift from component manufacturing toward full lifecycle sustainment for frontline fighters. Lucknow has long anchored HAL’s accessories ecosystem—landing gear, hydraulics, and braking systems—but the new shop is being configured for the demands of next-generation indigenous platforms and higher sortie rates.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Suryastra: Why the Indian Army’s ‘Universal’ Rocket Launcher is a Logistical Nightmare for the Enemy
item.description: The induction of the Suryastra system in January 2026 signals a doctrinal break in how the Indian Army conceptualizes rocket artillery. For decades, India’s multi-barrel rocket launcher (MBRL) ecosystem was defined by rigid, single-calibre platforms—Smerch units tied to 300 mm rockets and Pinaka regiments built around 214 mm munitions. This structure imposed not just operational inflexibility but also a heavy logistical burden, where each regiment required dedicated supply chains, storage, and mission planning cycles. Suryastra fundamentally dismantles that paradigm by introducing a universal, pod-based launcher architecture that collapses multiple roles into a single, modular system.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL still has chance to enter AMCA Program: Defence Secretary
item.description: Speaking on the evolving acquisition approach, Singh stressed the importance of avoiding overdependence on a single entity. He noted that “putting all your eggs in one basket is not a good idea,” emphasizing the need for limited competition even within strategic programs. While he clarified that the government would have been “perfectly happy” to have Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) involved in the AMCA from the outset, he also left the door open for HAL’s participation at a later stage, particularly during production. The current phase, however, remains focused on prototype development, where new industrial players are being actively considered.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: From Orbit to Doctrine: India’s 2026 Military Space Architecture and the Emergence of Integrated Counter-Space Posture
item.description: The year 2026 has crystallised a long-gestating shift: India’s space programme is no longer a civil scientific endeavour with incidental security applications but a fully integrated domain of military strategy. Revisions to the national space policy, accelerated military satellite constellations, and the formalisation of a Defence Space Command reflect lessons absorbed from recent operational experiences where space-based assets proved decisive for real-time targeting and battle damage assessment.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ICCS: The Real-Time Warfighting Brain Revolutionizing Multi-Domain Air Power Operations
item.description: In the high-stakes arena of modern warfare, where threats emerge from every direction and decisions must be instantaneous, the Integrated Command and Control System (ICCS) stands out as the ultimate real-time warfighting brain. This advanced network fuses sensors, data streams, and decision-making tools into a single, cohesive platform that empowers commanders to dominate the battlespace. At the recent Strategic Renaissance of Air Power Conference, Wing Commander Survesh Dhir delivered a compelling explanation of why ICCS is far more than a technical upgrade—it is the nervous system enabling faster, smarter responses across air, land, sea, and beyond.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Upgrading ZU-23-2 Guns into Anti-Drone Systems with EO/IR Sights and Fire Control Automation
item.description: The Indian Army has initiated a high-priority upgrade of its legacy ZU-23-2 air defence guns, transforming the 1960s-era manually operated system into a modern, all-weather counter-drone and low-altitude air defence platform. The move reflects a doctrinal shift driven by the growing threat of swarm drones and loitering munitions, particularly along sensitive frontiers such as the Line of Actual Control (LAC) and Line of Control (LoC).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Chief of Army’s Central Command reviews operational readiness of Shatrujeet Brigade
item.description: During the visit, he was briefed on the formation’s comprehensive training modules, operational preparedness, and the effective integration of niche technologies to enhance combat capability, according to an official statement.?
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Sapta Shakti Command hosts seminar on Iran war, geopolitical implications in Jaipur?
item.description: The event brought together a diverse audience of serving officers, veterans, academicians, and students from leading institutions across Jaipur. The seminar featured an eminent panel comprising former Chief of Air Staff Rakesh Kumar Singh Bhadauria, along with distinguished diplomats including Dinkar Srivastava, Gopal Baglay, and Satish Mehta.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: One Name, Many Modules: ISI Pushes ‘Ghazwa-E-Hind’ To Build Mass Movement In India
item.description: ISI-backed modules have been busted in recent months in Delhi, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha and Bihar. In each of these modules, the police found Ghazwa-e-Hind literature. An Intelligence Bureau official said that this looks like a pattern. Although the modules may not be inter-linked, the one thing they had in common was this slogan and also the ISI link.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: 2,922 Seafarers Back So Far, Indian-Flagged Vessels Safe In Gulf Region: Govt
item.description: “All Indian seafarers in the region are safe, and no incident involving Indian-flagged vessels has been reported in the past 24 hours. DG Shipping Control Room has handled 8,335 calls and more than 17,838 emails since activation. In the past 24 hours, 67 calls and 144 emails have been received,” said the ministry in its regular update.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Unmatched valour of soldiers marked Op Sindoor as a golden chapter in India’s military history: Raksha Mantri at Shaurya
item.description: Raksha Mantri commended the valour, dedication and patriotism of the soldiers, and stated that their ethos of Nation First & Service Before Self have defined India’s military traditions from ancient times to contemporary operations such as Op Sindoor. He added that the valour of the Defence Forces extends beyond the battlefield as they provide humanitarian assistance as first responders during natural disasters within the country & abroad. “Our soldiers use weapons to protect their people, and deliver food & medicines to those in need during the times of crises. This is the defining characteristic of our Defence Forces,” he said.
item.dc:creator: PIB

item.title: India’s new envoy to China arrives in Shanghai
item.description: He was received at the airport by the Indian Consul General in Shanghai Pratik Mathur and other officials. Doraiswami, a 1992-batch Indian Foreign Service officer, is due to arrive in Beijing on Sunday. In Beijing, the 56-year-old diplomat succeeds Pradeep Kumar Rawat.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: 4 police personnel killed as IED goes off during demining operation in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker
item.description: This was the first blast incident linked to Naxalite activity in the state since it was declared free from armed Maoism on March 31. It was also the first instance this year in which security personnel engaged in anti-Naxal operations have lost their lives.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Man arrested in Gujarat’s Kutch for ISIS links, spreading extremist content online
item.description: Preliminary investigations indicate that the accused had been operating multiple fake identities on social media platforms and was allegedly sharing provocative and inflammatory content aimed at inciting individuals towards “jihad”.
item.dc:creator: IANS
